name: opt-next3
author: lordshashank
notes: Supersedes opt-next2 (all its changes included: arm64 asm on all aarch64, signed-Booth legacy MSM, deep point prefetch, consume-polynomials, split pow table) and adds the memory campaign, attacking the PK-construction transient that sets the process peak. (1) **sigma/id as u32 sidecars**: every sigma/id value is +/-FF(k) for k < 2^31 (SEPARATOR = 2^28 separates rows/wires/tags; public-input overrides are negated small indices), captured exactly by a u32 with a sign bit. The permutation argument is computed u32-first while copy-cycle data is alive, cycles are dropped, and only then are the Fr images materialized; after sumcheck's first-round fold (now entity-interleaved allocate+fold), the Fr originals are released and the Gemini rho-batching pass reads the sidecars with the rho power of their original slot - bit-identical values, ~384 MiB released at 4 bytes/element kept. (2) **Flat CSR copy cycles**: the per-variable vector-of-vectors costs millions of ~32-byte heap chunks that inflate the live footprint and leave the glibc arena fragmented exactly where the sigma/id materialization allocates; two flat arrays (-160 MiB peak measured). (3) **Wide selectors trimmed to nonzero support** after trace population: q_m is all-zero on this trace (46 MB of zeros), q_c/q_r/q_o/q_4 mostly zero; reads outside the window hit virtual zeros, values identical. (4) z_perm allocation deferred to its first write (the grand product), malloc_trim before the materialization climb. Linux-arm64 container A/B (CI toolchain recipe): baseline 31.91s/1983MiB -> 21.7-22.0s/1454-1468MiB = approx -32% time, -26% peak RSS. RSS is now flat from PK-complete to the last KZG commit. Proofs verify against the pinned baseline VK; all 291 ultra_honk tests pass; claims below are Apple M-series grade.mjs --runs=5 (advisory; macOS RSS carries ambient noise). See https://github.com/lordshashank/aztec-packages/pull/4 and RESEARCH.md.
